# User and Host Management

## TUMonline Overview

**TUMonline** is TUM's central campus management system and the primary platform for managing users, groups, and organizational data. It provides a comprehensive interface for IT administrators to manage their department's IT resources.

### Access Information

| Property | Value |
|----------|-------|
| **URL** | [campus.tum.de](https://campus.tum.de) |
| **Access** | Role-based permissions for authorized administrators |
| **Documentation** | [TUM Docs Wiki](https://wiki.tum.de) with illustrated guides |

### Training Resources

TUMonline offers extensive training materials:

| Resource | Audience | Format |
|----------|----------|--------|
| **TUM Docs Wiki** | All users | Illustrated step-by-step guides |
| **Video Tutorials** | Role holders (coordinators, guest admins) | Video |
| **YouTube Screencasts** | Students | Video |
| **"IT-Ratgeber" Brochure** | Students | PDF guide |
| **"TUMonline: Einführung für Mitarbeitende"** | Employees | Brochure |
| **"TUMonline Basics"** | Employees | Video tutorial |
| **On-Demand Training** | Administrators | Scheduled sessions |

### Organizational Structure

TUMonline administration follows a **Keyuser structure** supported by the Campus Management Team:

- **TUMonline-Beauftragte** (TUMonline coordinators) at each organizational unit
- **Keyuser areas** for specialized management functions
- **Guest administrators** for managing external users

## Core Functions

```{mermaid}
%%| fig-cap: "TUMonline Core Functions for IT Admins"
mindmap
    root((TUMonline))
        User Management
            Account Administration
            Access Permissions
            Identity Management
        Group Management
            Organizational Groups
            Mailing Lists
            Permission Sets
        Function Objects
            Shared Mailboxes
            Group Calendars
            Room Management
            Projector Booking
        Integration
            LDAP Directory
            Active Directory
            Exchange
```

## User Management

### User Account Lifecycle

```{mermaid}
%%| fig-cap: "User Account Lifecycle"
sequenceDiagram
    participant Admin as Administrator
    participant TUMo as TUMonline
    participant LDAP as TUM LDAP / AD
    participant User as New User

    Admin->>TUMo: Manage user account
    TUMo->>TUMo: Process changes
    TUMo->>LDAP: Synchronize identity
    LDAP-->>TUMo: Confirmation
    TUMo-->>Admin: Account updated
    Admin->>User: Communicate access details
    User->>LDAP: Login with TUM credentials
```

User management tasks include:

1. Managing user accounts and permissions via TUMonline
2. Assigning users to organizational groups
3. Configuring access levels for departmental resources
4. Handling account changes when users join or leave

### Managing User Groups

Groups in TUMonline serve multiple purposes:

| Group Type | Purpose | Example Use |
|------------|---------|-------------|
| **Organizational Groups** | Map organizational structure | Department members |
| **Access Groups** | Control resource access | Printer access, file shares |
| **Mailing Lists** | Email distribution | Department announcements |
| **LDAP Groups** | Directory integration | Authentication and authorization |

#### Creating Groups

1. Navigate to Group Management in TUMonline
2. Select "Create New Group"
3. Define group properties:
   - Group name (following naming conventions)
   - Description
   - Initial members
   - Group type
4. Save and activate the group

### LDAP Integration

```{mermaid}
%%| fig-cap: "LDAP Integration Flow"
flowchart LR
    TUMo["TUMonline"]
    TUMo --> |sync| LDAP["TUM Central LDAP"]
    LDAP --> Auth["Authentication<br/>Services"]
    LDAP --> NAS["NAS Access<br/>Control"]
    LDAP --> AD["Active<br/>Directory"]
    LDAP --> Mail["Mail<br/>Services"]
```

Groups and users managed through TUMonline are automatically:

- Synchronized with the central TUM LDAP directory
- Available for authentication across TUM IT services
- Used for access control on storage, servers, and other resources

## Host Management

### Registering Devices

Administrators manage device registration for network access:

```{mermaid}
%%| fig-cap: "Device Registration Workflow"
flowchart TB
    subgraph Registration["Device Registration"]
        Device["New Device"]
        Device --> Info["Collect Information"]
        Info --> |MAC Address<br/>Device Type<br/>Location| Register["Network Registration"]
    end

    subgraph Configuration["Network Configuration"]
        Register --> DNS["DNS Entry"]
        Register --> DHCP["DHCP Reservation"]
        Register --> Inventory["Inventory Record"]
    end
```

#### Required Information for Host Registration

| Field | Description | Example |
|-------|-------------|---------|
| **Hostname** | Unique device name | `ws-mueller-01` |
| **MAC Address** | Network interface ID | `00:1A:2B:3C:4D:5E` |
| **Device Type** | Category | Laptop, Desktop, Printer, Server |
| **Location** | Physical location | Room 01.234, Building |
| **Responsible Person** | Primary user/contact | Prof. Dr. Schmidt |

### Infrastructure Inventory

Administrators should maintain a complete inventory of:

- **Laptops**: Mobile workstations assigned to users
- **Desktops**: Fixed workstations in offices and labs
- **Printers**: Network printers and multifunction devices
- **Servers**: Both virtual and physical servers
- **Network Equipment**: Managed switches and access points

## Email Services

### TUM Email Address

All TUM members (students, employees, guests) automatically receive a personal TUM email address:

| Feature | Description |
|---------|-------------|
| **Availability** | Automatic for all TUM members |
| **Lifetime Forwarding** | Can be used as a forwarding address for life, even after leaving TUM |
| **Format** | firstname.lastname@tum.de |

::: {.callout-tip}
## Lifetime Forwarding
The TUM email address serves as a permanent forwarding address. Even after leaving TUM, former members can continue using it to forward emails to their new address.
:::

### Exchange Services

Microsoft Exchange is TUM's central email platform providing comprehensive email and collaboration:

```{mermaid}
%%| fig-cap: "Exchange Service Features"
mindmap
    root((Exchange))
        Email
            Personal Mailbox
            Shared Mailboxes
            Distribution Lists
            S/MIME Signing
        Calendar
            Personal Calendar
            Group Calendars
            Room Booking
            Meeting Requests
        Access Methods
            Outlook Desktop
            Outlook Web App
            IMAP Clients
            Mobile Devices
        Collaboration
            Shared Contacts
            Tasks
            Notes
```

| Feature | Description |
|---------|-------------|
| **Email** | Full email functionality with storage quota |
| **Calendar** | Personal and shared calendars, room booking, meeting automation |
| **Shared Mailboxes** | Team email addresses |
| **Access** | Outlook, Outlook Web App (OWA), IMAP clients, mobile sync |
| **Security** | S/MIME email signing and encryption |
| **Linux** | Access via IMAP clients or web interface |
| **Mass Mailing** | Daily sending limits apply |

::: {.callout-note}
## Email Encryption with S/MIME
S/MIME allows signing and encrypting emails. Both sender and recipient must have S/MIME certificates for encrypted communication. User certificates are available through TUM's DFN-PKI portal (see Chapter 5 - Certificates).
:::

### Mailing Lists (Mailverteiler)

TUM provides three distinct mailing list solutions:

```{mermaid}
%%| fig-cap: "Mailing List Options"
flowchart TB
    Need["Need a Mailing List?"]
    Need --> Q1{"TUM members<br/>only?"}

    Q1 --> |Yes| Q2{"Dynamic<br/>membership?"}
    Q1 --> |No| Mailman["Mailman (LRZ)<br/>External recipients supported"]

    Q2 --> |Yes| FO["TUMonline<br/>Funktionsobjekte<br/>(auto-updated)"]
    Q2 --> |No| Direct["TUMonline<br/>Direct Distribution"]
```

#### 1. TUMonline Funktionsobjekte (Dynamic Lists)

| Feature | Description |
|---------|-------------|
| **Type** | Dynamic mailing lists that stay current automatically |
| **Visibility** | Appear in TUM-wide Exchange address book |
| **Members** | Must be active TUM members (external addresses possible as exception) |
| **Management** | Via "Funktionsobjekte-Verwalter" role in TUMonline |
| **Best for** | Ongoing department/team communication |

#### 2. TUMonline Direct Email Distribution

| Feature | Description |
|---------|-------------|
| **Type** | Send emails directly to selected target groups |
| **Filtering** | Recipients can be filtered by TUMonline attributes (position, department, etc.) |
| **Best for** | One-time or irregular mass communications |

#### 3. Mailman (LRZ)

| Feature | Description |
|---------|-------------|
| **Type** | Traditional mailing list and newsletter system |
| **Provider** | Free service from LRZ |
| **External** | Supports both TUM members AND external partners |
| **Subscription** | Mandatory Double-Opt-In registration |
| **Best for** | Newsletters, communication with external recipients |

::: {.callout-tip}
## Automatic List Management
Use TUMonline Funktionsobjekte for dynamic lists that update automatically as people join or leave organizational units. For communication with external parties, use Mailman via LRZ.
:::

#### Faculty Mailing List Coordinators

Each faculty has a designated **Mailverteiler VerwalterIn** (mailing list administrator) who can assist with list creation and management. For TUM-wide campaigns, contact:

- **Staff communications**: Corporate Communication Center
- **Student communications**: Student Service Center

### Shared Mailboxes and Calendars (Funktionsobjekte)

TUMonline Function Objects (Funktionsobjekte) are shared resources for improving organizational collaboration:

| Feature | Description |
|---------|-------------|
| **Shared Mailboxes** | Team email addresses (e.g., info@department.tum.de) |
| **Group Calendars** | Shared scheduling |
| **Room Management** | Room and resource booking |
| **Projector Booking** | Equipment reservation |
| **Management** | Via TUMonline Function Objects |
| **Training** | Video training sessions available for administrators |

::: {.callout-note}
## Function Objects vs. Mailing Lists

| Feature | Mailing List | Function Object (Shared Mailbox) |
|---------|--------------|----------------------------------|
| **Receive emails** | All members | All members |
| **Send as shared address** | No | Yes |
| **Shared inbox** | No | Yes |
| **Calendar** | No | Yes |
| **Room/Resource booking** | No | Yes |
| **Best for** | Announcements | Team collaboration |
:::

## TUMonline Integration

### Overview

TUMonline is TUM's campus management system and integrates with many IT services:

```{mermaid}
%%| fig-cap: "TUMonline Service Integration"
flowchart TB
    TUMonline["TUMonline"]

    TUMonline --> Identity["Identity Management"]
    TUMonline --> Email["Email Services"]
    TUMonline --> Groups["Group Management"]
    TUMonline --> Functions["Function Objects"]
    TUMonline --> SysAcc["System Accounts"]

    Identity --> AD["Active Directory"]
    Identity --> LDAP["LDAP Directory"]
    Email --> Exchange["Exchange"]
    Groups --> Lists["Mailing Lists"]
    Functions --> Shared["Shared Mailboxes<br/>& Calendars"]
    SysAcc --> Devices["Printers, Scanners,<br/>Automated Services"]
```

### System Accounts

System Accounts (Systemkennung) are non-personal credentials for devices and automated services:

| Feature | Description |
|---------|-------------|
| **Purpose** | Enable printers, scanners, and services to use TUM IT services |
| **Exchange** | Do NOT appear in the TUM Exchange address book |
| **Email** | NOT email mailboxes -- emails are redirected to the responsible person |
| **Request** | Via online form in TUMonline (specify email redirection in remarks) |

::: {.callout-important}
## System Accounts vs. Shared Mailboxes
System Accounts cannot receive emails. If you need a shared email address (e.g., sekretariat@xyz.tum.de), request a **Shared Mailbox** via Function Objects instead.
:::

### Administrator Resources

| Resource | Purpose |
|----------|---------|
| **TUMonline Portal** | User and group management |
| **TUM Docs Wiki** | Illustrated guides and tutorials |
| **TUMonline-Beauftragte** | Local TUMonline coordinators |
| **Keyuser Areas** | Specialized management areas |
| **Video Tutorials** | Training for role holders |

## Best Practices

### User Account Management

- **Regular Audits**: Periodically review active accounts
- **Timely Deactivation**: Remove accounts when users leave
- **Appropriate Permissions**: Apply principle of least privilege
- **Documentation**: Keep records of special access grants

### Group Management

- **Naming Conventions**: Follow established naming patterns
- **Clear Descriptions**: Document group purpose and membership criteria
- **Regular Review**: Audit group memberships periodically
- **Cleanup**: Remove obsolete groups

### Infrastructure Records

- **Complete Information**: Fill all relevant fields
- **Current Data**: Update records when devices move or change
- **Disposal Tracking**: Mark devices as decommissioned when appropriate
